userMessage: the session file did not load properly

response.text:
The session did load this time, but not completely.

What I found:
- I set the scope working directory to C:/tests.
- I confirmed the file exists there:
  - baseline.tss
- I recalled the session with:
  - RECAll:SESsion "C:/tests/baseline.tss"
- The recall completed with a warning, not a clean restore:
  - 2501, "Setup warning, could not recall all values from external setup; baseline_lrn.set"

What the current display shows now:
- Left side:
  - Spectrum View heatmap on top
  - Spectrum trace below
  - Center frequency about 98.000000 MHz
  - Span 10.0000 MHz
  - RBW 10.00 kHz
- Right side:
  - Waveform View of CH1 only
  - Dense yellow filled trace
- Measurement area:
  - Only Meas 1 is present
  - Type: Period
  - Mean about 37.74 ns
- Previous items are gone:
  - no harmonics plot
  - no eye diagram
  - no Meas 4
  - no Power 1 harmonics panel

So your diagnosis was correct: the earlier load did not restore the intended session contents. The scope itself reported that some values could not be recalled from an external setup file, which explains why the display came up differently.

The specific issue reported by the instrument earlier was:
- invalid enumeration tied to:
  - PLOT:PLOT2:SOUrce1 MEAS4
This strongly suggests the saved session referenced a measurement/plot source that was not valid when the session was restored, so parts of the old layout were skipped.

What I did successfully:
- verified file presence
- reloaded the session from C:/tests/baseline.tss
- confirmed the scope warning after recall
- captured the updated screen showing the partially restored spectrum-view session

If you want, I can now try to inspect baseline_lrn.set / related files and determine exactly what part of the session reference is broken.
